title: No atmospheric sky effects visible — world appears gray/colorless
description: When running the game, the sky appears gray or black instead of showing a blue sky with Rayleigh scattering from the Bevy Atmosphere component. The world looks colorless despite Atmosphere::earthlike(), ClearColor::BLACK, and DistanceFog all being configured.
reproduction: Run `cargo run --features bevy/dynamic_linking`. Look up — sky should be blue with sun-driven scattering but appears flat gray.
suspected_cause: Possible causes: (1) Atmosphere component requires specific plugin ordering or camera HDR mode not being satisfied at runtime, (2) ClearColor::BLACK may not be interacting correctly with the atmosphere shader, (3) DirectionalLight (Sun) may not be positioned/rotated correctly for the atmosphere to compute sky color, (4) ScatteringMedium asset may not be loaded before camera spawn.
notes: Code investigation shows all components are present: Atmosphere::earthlike(medium), ScatteringMedium::default(), ClearColor::BLACK, DistanceFog, Bloom::NATURAL (for HDR).
AtmospherePlugin is auto-registered by PbrPlugin — no extra plugin needed.
Atmosphere requires HDR on the camera — Bloom::NATURAL should satisfy this but verify at runtime.
Try running with RUST_LOG=bevy_pbr=debug to check for atmosphere shader warnings.

title: Deformed terrain geometry — visible surface distortion
description: Terrain surfaces appear visually deformed or warped. The Surface Nets mesh output may have incorrect vertex positions, normals, or quad connectivity causing visible distortion.
reproduction: Run the game and observe terrain at various distances. Look for warped/stretched surfaces, especially at chunk boundaries and LOD transitions.
suspected_cause: Possible causes: (1) LOD stride mismatch — when LOD level > 0, Surface Nets uses stride = 2^level which coarsens geometry; transitions between LOD levels may create visible seams, (2) Chunk boundary stitching may have edge cases where neighbor snapshots are missing (treats boundary as air), (3) Noise values at terrain surface may produce ambiguous isosurface crossings, (4) Vertex normal computation may be wrong for steep terrain.
notes: Surface Nets implementation has proper boundary handling via 6 face-neighbor snapshots.
LOD hysteresis (12% dead-band) should prevent thrashing but may not eliminate all visual artifacts.
Integration tests validate chunk stitching (meshing.rs:1114, 1401) — issue may be data-dependent.
Check if deformation correlates with LOD transitions by temporarily disabling LOD (force level 0).

title: Degenerate geometry visible inside terrain
description: When looking at terrain from certain angles or through gaps, degenerate/invisible geometry is visible inside the terrain volume. This suggests the meshing algorithm is generating internal faces that should be culled, or there are holes in the surface mesh revealing interior artifacts.
reproduction: Run the game. Fly through terrain (G for fly mode) or look at terrain edges/overhangs. Internal geometry may be visible as flickering faces or z-fighting artifacts.
suspected_cause: Possible causes: (1) Surface Nets generating quads for internal isosurface crossings (e.g., cave surfaces inside solid rock), (2) Backface culling not enabled — internal faces visible from outside, (3) Octree subdivision creating surface artifacts at resolution boundaries, (4) Chunk overlap regions producing duplicate geometry.
notes: Surface Nets should only generate surface mesh at the air/solid boundary. If internal geometry exists, the algorithm may be finding false surface crossings.
Check if StandardMaterial has cull_mode set to Back (Bevy default is Back).
Cave carving may create internal air pockets that generate visible but unreachable surfaces.
Octree leaf-level differences could create T-junctions or cracks.

title: In-game map (M key) does not open
description: Pressing M during gameplay does not open the in-game map overlay. The map system (src/map/) was implemented with M key toggle, Tab for local/global switch, and ESC to close, but none of these appear to function at runtime.
reproduction: Run `cargo run --features bevy/dynamic_linking`. During gameplay (Playing state), press M. Map overlay should appear but does not.
suspected_cause: Possible causes: (1) toggle_map system may have a .run_if() condition that prevents it from running in the Playing state, (2) GameState transition from Playing → Map may not trigger OnEnter(GameState::Map), (3) KeyCode::KeyM may not match the physical key on all keyboard layouts, (4) MapPlugin may not be registered or may conflict with another input handler consuming the M key.
notes: Code investigation shows MapPlugin IS registered in main.rs.
toggle_map system listens for KeyCode::KeyM with just_pressed().
System has .run_if(in_state(GameState::Playing).or(in_state(GameState::Map))) — verify this compiles and runs.
Add temporary debug logging to toggle_map to confirm the system is executing.

title: Function keys not working or not matching documentation
description: Function keys (F1–F12) either do not respond or produce unexpected behavior compared to what documentation describes. Known bindings: F3 (debug overlay), F5 (ECS state dump), F9 (toggle chunk borders), F11 (fullscreen), F12 (screenshot).
reproduction: Run the game. Press F3, F5, F9, F11, F12 individually. Compare behavior to README.md and diagnostics docs.
suspected_cause: Possible causes: (1) F12 has DUPLICATE screenshot_system functions — one in diagnostics/mod.rs:260 and one in planet/render.rs:615, which may cause conflicts, (2) Some F-key systems may only run in specific GameStates (e.g., only Playing, not WorldCreation), (3) Desktop environment (Wayland) may intercept certain F-keys before they reach the application, (4) DiagnosticsPlugin may not be registered or may have incorrect run conditions.
notes: Code investigation confirmed: F3, F5, F9, F11 are all properly bound in diagnostics/mod.rs.
F12 screenshot has CONFIRMED duplicate function definitions — this is a real bug that needs fixing.
Wayland compositors (Sway, Hyprland, etc.) may capture F-keys for their own bindings — check compositor config.
Test with WINIT_UNIX_BACKEND=x11 to rule out Wayland-specific key capture.

title: Game crashes during play
description: The game crashes at some point during gameplay. Specific crash conditions, timing, and error messages not yet documented.
reproduction: Run `RUST_BACKTRACE=1 cargo run --features bevy/dynamic_linking` and play until crash occurs. Capture the full backtrace.
suspected_cause: Possible causes: (1) terrain.rs:385 has a fragile unwrap() on env.as_ref() — logically safe but could panic if use_advanced flag gets out of sync, (2) Chunk loading/meshing may panic on edge cases (out-of-bounds voxel access), (3) Asset loading race conditions (accessing materials/textures before they're loaded), (4) Stack overflow from deep recursion in octree operations.
notes: CRITICAL: Need user to provide backtrace from `RUST_BACKTRACE=1 cargo run --features bevy/dynamic_linking`.
terrain.rs:385 unwrap() is the most likely candidate — should be hardened regardless.
No other unsafe unwrap() patterns found in hot paths (Update/FixedUpdate systems).
Once backtrace is available, update this issue with the specific panic location.
